title: HarnessConfig
description: Per-project configuration for Claude Code Harness. Lives at <projectRoot>/harness.config.json.

projectName.description: Human-readable project name shown in messages.

protectedDirectories.description: Directory names that R10 refuses to delete via rm/rmdir/unlink. Empty array disables R10.

protectedEnvVarNames.description: Environment-variable names R11 blocks in Bash commands. Empty array disables R11.

protectedFileSuffixes.description: File suffixes whose direct read (cat/head/tail/less/more/open/echo) R13 blocks.

sync.description: Tunables for the codex-sync agent. Mitigates mid-response truncation when Claude Code's TASK_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH (default 32000, documented max 160000) is exceeded by a Codex review output. `harness doctor` uses these thresholds to warn the user when the effective env var is below the runtime default without hardcoding the limit. See agents/codex-sync.md for the user-facing remediation path.

recommendedTaskMaxOutputLength.description: Value `harness doctor` prints as the recommended setting for TASK_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH. Claude Code silently clamps higher values so advertising one would be misleading.

warnTaskMaxOutputLengthBelow.description: Threshold below which `harness doctor` flags the current effective TASK_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH as WARN. Default mirrors Claude Code's runtime default so the doctor warns whenever the env var is unset.

checkTaskMaxOutputLength.description: When true, `harness doctor` reports the effective TASK_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH alongside codex plugin detection. Set false to silence the report entirely.

work.description: Settings consumed by /harness-work, /tdd-implement, /parallel-worktree, and core hooks (stop / subagent-stop / pre-compact / task-lifecycle).

plansFile.description: Relative path to the project's task / plan file. Consumed by pre-compact and task-lifecycle hooks to read assignment tables before compaction.

assignmentSectionMarkers.description: Section header keywords used to locate the assignment table inside the plans file. Supports ja/en projects. First-match wins.

handoffFiles.description: Project-specific session-handoff file paths that /harness-work and /parallel-worktree update when closing a session. Existence-optional (if a listed path is missing, the hook skips it silently).

changeLogFile.description: Optional relative path to a project-local change log consumed by /harness-plan sync. If unset, sync skips change-log scanning.

testCommand.description: Canonical test command surfaced to /harness-work and /harness-release as their test step when set. Consumed by the orchestrator commands, **not by the Stop / SubagentStop hooks** (those hooks still rely on per-stack autodetection via detectAvailableChecks()). Example: 'pytest -q' for a Python project, 'npm test' for a Node project.

qualityGates.description: Per-project override of harness quality gates. Consumed by stop.ts to emit reminders.

security.description: Settings consumed by security-auditor agent. Lets projects supply their own stack-specific security checklist without modifying plugin core.

projectChecklistPath.description: Relative path to a project-local security checklist (e.g. `./AGENTS.md`, `./docs/security.md`, or a SKILL.md reference). security-auditor agent loads this file as addendum when present; otherwise runs the stack-neutral generic checklist only.

enabledChecks.description: Enabled security check categories. `project-specific` is effective only when `projectChecklistPath` is set.

pseudoCoderabbitProfile.description: CodeRabbit 公式 profile は chill/assertive のみ。strict は harness-local 拡張。

tooling.description: Stack-detection knobs consumed by core hooks (e.g. subagent-stop.ts's detectAvailableChecks). Stack-neutral defaults are deliberately generic; project-specific layouts (like `backend/`) are opted in via override.

pythonCandidateDirs.description: Directory names checked for Python source when picking lint/typecheck targets. A directory is included only if it exists at `projectRoot`. Each entry must match `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$/` (alphanumerics plus `_` / `-` / `.` — no path separators, whitespace, or shell metacharacters); rejected entries are warned about and dropped. Default omits `backend/` to avoid forcing a layout assumption; projects with a `backend/` tree add it explicitly. Edge cases: an **empty array** `[]` or any non-string / empty-string entry is treated as a malformed override and falls back to the default (this is safe — it never produces an unexpected empty lint target). To intentionally skip Python linting entirely, list a directory name that does **not exist** in the repo (e.g. `[".no-python-lint"]`); the existence check filters it out and ruff / mypy are skipped. Listing an existing empty directory does **not** skip linting — the tool will still run against it and find no Python files.

release.description: Settings consumed by /harness-release, /branch-merge, and /new-feature-branch. Projects that follow a two-branch (main only) or three-branch (main + dev + feature/*) flow surface their choice here so the plugin can stay stack/branch-neutral.

strategy.description: Branch-merge strategy. `three-branch` = feature/* → dev → main (default, matches /branch-merge spec). `two-branch` = feature/* → main directly (simpler trunk-style repos).

integrationBranch.description: Intermediate branch name in the `three-branch` strategy. Ignored when `strategy` = `two-branch`.

productionBranch.description: Final / production branch name. Used by /branch-merge and /harness-release for tag placement.

testCommand.description: Command run before each merge step. When unset, /harness-release falls back to `work.testCommand` and finally to per-stack autodetection.

userPromptSubmit.description: Settings consumed by the UserPromptSubmit hook. Acts as the harness Global plugin → Local project rules bridge: each user prompt is augmented with project-local context files (e.g. `.claude/rules/coding-style.md`) so Claude inherits invariants the project owner cares about, without forcing the user to re-paste them. Empty `contextFiles` (default) makes the hook a no-op.

contextFiles.description: Project-local context file paths (relative to projectRoot) appended to each user prompt as `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`. Missing files are skipped silently. Path-traversal entries (containing `..`) and absolute paths are rejected and skipped.

maxTotalBytes.description: Total byte cap across all contextFiles. Excess content is truncated with an inline marker so Claude can detect the boundary.

fenceContext.description: When true, the injected context block is wrapped in fence markers with a per-request 12-hex-char nonce (`===== HARNESS PROJECT-LOCAL CONTEXT <nonce> =====` / `===== END HARNESS CONTEXT <nonce> =====`) so the user / reader can attribute the content to the harness. The nonce defeats context-boundary spoofing attempts by literal fence markers embedded in rule file content.

postToolUseFailure.description: Settings consumed by the PostToolUseFailure hook. Fires when a tool execution fails (exception / non-zero exit / interrupt). Injects failure diagnostics and optional corrective hints into `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` so Claude can decide next-turn recovery without extra prompting.

enabled.description: If false, the hook returns approve with no additionalContext (silent skip). Default true.

maxErrorLength.description: Maximum length of the raw error string before truncation. Excess is cut with an inline marker.

correctiveHints.description: When true, append harness built-in corrective hints for well-known error patterns (permission denied / no such file / command not found / signal abort / timeout / network unreachable). Set false to inject raw error only.

models.description: Model registry consumed by every harness-dispatched Codex invocation (`codex-sync` / `codex-team` / `coderabbit-mimic` / `tdd-implement`). When this section is omitted the resolver reports `source: "harness-default"` (compile-time fallback `HARNESS_DEFAULT_MODEL` in `src/models/resolver.ts`). Use `codex.default` for a single harness-wide pin, `codex.aliases` to name logical tiers, and `agents.*.model` / `agents.*.reasoningEffort` to override per Codex agent. Schema-level `default` annotations are deliberately omitted so schema-aware editors do not pre-populate these values — absence is semantically meaningful (distinguishes shipped behaviour from explicit pin).

codex.description: Harness-level Codex model settings applied when no per-agent override exists.

default.description: Codex model slug served by the Codex CLI (e.g. a `gpt-X.Y` or `gpt-X.Y-codex` variant). May reference a logical name declared in `aliases`. Resolved by `harness model resolve <agent>` and injected as `codex exec --model <slug>` at agent invocation time. When omitted the resolver falls back to `HARNESS_DEFAULT_MODEL`.

reasoningEffort.description: `codex exec --effort` value. The 5 levels match the Codex config-reference documentation. Per-agent overrides under `agents.*.reasoningEffort` take precedence. When omitted the resolver falls back to `HARNESS_DEFAULT_REASONING_EFFORT`.

aliases.description: Logical-name → concrete-model-slug map so projects can say `default: "strong"` / `default: "fast"` instead of hardcoding the slug everywhere. Alias lookup runs once at resolve-time; circular or missing aliases return the literal name (surfaced by `harness model check --strict` as `unknown-slug`).

agents.description: Per-agent model overrides. Keys are agent names as understood by the resolver (`codex-sync`, `codex-team`, `coderabbit-mimic`; the resolver strips the `harness:` prefix and `.md` suffix before lookup). An entry without `model` simply inherits `codex.default`.

model.description: Overrides `codex.default` for this agent. Can reference an alias.
